issues.detail: The required causal flow—"When the volunteer marks the PRD ready, an automated scorer compares it" and a passing score "decomposes the build backlog"—is split between a scorer test and a gate test that begins with an already-supplied score. A system requiring a separate/manual gate invocation could pass both.
issues.fix: Add P0 end-to-end passing and failing scenarios that perform only the volunteer's mark-ready action, then assert the resulting score event automatically drives the correct gate branch.

issues.detail: "It consumes project fuel (a usage row exists for the scoring run)" treats a usage row as evidence of fuel consumption. REQ-034 explicitly separates the token-denominated usage log from provider-authoritative money/fuel accounting, so the stated observation cannot prove fuel was billed.
issues.fix: Assert an authoritative project-fuel consumption entry or corresponding balance change for the scoring run; verify the usage row separately if attribution coverage is also intended.

issues.detail: "More attempts than any plausible cap" has no defined attempt count or pass/fail boundary, and a finite run alone cannot establish that no scorer-specific attempt cap exists.
issues.fix: Use a specified attempt count derived from configured limits, verify every pre-fuel-stop attempt succeeds, inspect/assert absence of scorer-specific attempt-limit state or configuration, and confirm the first rejection is caused only by the fuel stop.

issues.detail: The clause "NGO input is via clarifications" is not fully covered. AT-036.09 checks only that approve/reject controls are absent; it would still pass if the NGO could edit or mark the PRD ready, trigger re-scoring, or override the score, threshold, or gate through another UI/API route.
issues.fix: Add an NGO-role test proving clarification responses are allowed while every PRD-authoring, readiness, scoring, threshold, and gate mutation route is absent or authorization-denied and creates no score or backlog event.
